<!--
Copyright (c) 2026 Dhruv Azad. All rights reserved.
Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Dhruv Azad
-->
---
title: "About"
page-layout: full
---

<div class="about-hero">

<div class="about-hero-content">

<p class="about-eyebrow">About the project</p>

<h1>About VisualMath</h1>

<p class="about-hero-subtitle">
VisualMath is a collection of interactive computational mathematics apps designed to help
students see numerical algorithms unfold step by step.
</p>

<p class="about-hero-text">
Instead of treating algorithms as static formulas, VisualMath turns them into dynamic
visual experiences with controls, plots, iteration tables, and explanations.
</p>

</div>

<div class="about-hero-card">
  <div class="about-hero-card-label">Core idea</div>
  <div class="about-hero-card-formula">
    $\text{formula} \quad + \quad \text{visualization} \quad + \quad \text{interaction}$
  </div>
  <p>
    Change the inputs, step through the algorithm, and connect the computation to the picture.
  </p>
</div>

</div>

<div class="about-section about-mission-section">

<div class="about-section-header">
  <p class="about-eyebrow">Purpose</p>
  <h2>Making numerical methods visible</h2>
</div>

<div class="about-two-column">

<div>

Many algorithms in computational mathematics are iterative. They begin with an initial
guess, interval, or approximation, and then improve it step by step.

VisualMath is designed to make that process visible. Students can interact with the
method, change inputs, and watch how the algorithm moves.

For example, instead of only seeing Newton's update formula,

$$
x_{n+1}=x_n-\frac{f(x_n)}{f'(x_n)},
$$

students can see the tangent line, the current point, the next approximation, and the
iteration table all update together.

</div>

<div class="about-callout">
  <h3>Learning by experimenting</h3>
  <p>
    The apps are built so students can ask “what if?” questions:
    What happens if the starting point changes? What if the interval is bad?
    What if the function is flat near a root?
  </p>
</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="about-section">

<div class="about-section-header">
  <p class="about-eyebrow">Current modules</p>
  <h2>What is available now?</h2>
</div>

<div class="about-card-grid">

<div class="about-info-card">
  <div class="about-card-icon">√</div>
  <h3>Root Finding</h3>
  <p>
    The root finding module shows numerical methods for solving equations of the form
    \(f(x)=0\). Current methods include bisection, fixed point iteration, and Newton's method.
  </p>
  <a href="root-finding/" class="about-card-link">Explore root finding</a>
</div>

<div class="about-info-card">
  <div class="about-card-icon">↻</div>
  <h3>Interactive Visualization</h3>
  <p>
    Each app combines controls, plots, status messages, and iteration tables so students
    can connect the algebraic formula with the geometric picture.
  </p>
</div>

<div class="about-info-card">
  <div class="about-card-icon">⚡</div>
  <h3>Fast Static Apps</h3>
  <p>
    The apps use browser-native interactivity instead of a Python runtime in the browser.
    This keeps the site fast, responsive, and easy to deploy.
  </p>
</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="about-section about-goals-section">

<div class="about-section-header">
  <p class="about-eyebrow">Design goals</p>
  <h2>Built for clarity and exploration</h2>
</div>

<div class="about-goal-list">

<div class="about-goal-item">
  <span>01</span>
  <p>Make computational mathematics more visual and approachable.</p>
</div>

<div class="about-goal-item">
  <span>02</span>
  <p>Show algorithms step by step instead of only presenting final answers.</p>
</div>

<div class="about-goal-item">
  <span>03</span>
  <p>Allow students to experiment with different functions, intervals, and starting values.</p>
</div>

<div class="about-goal-item">
  <span>04</span>
  <p>Keep the website lightweight, fast, and easy to deploy as a static site.</p>
</div>

<div class="about-goal-item">
  <span>05</span>
  <p>Provide clear explanations below each interactive app.</p>
</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="about-section">

<div class="about-section-header">
  <p class="about-eyebrow">Technology</p>
  <h2>How VisualMath is built</h2>
</div>

<div class="tech-grid">

<div class="tech-card">
  <h3>Quarto</h3>
  <p>Provides the website structure, pages, navigation, and publishing workflow.</p>
</div>

<div class="tech-card">
  <h3>OJS</h3>
  <p>Powers the reactive browser-side interactivity used by the apps.</p>
</div>

<div class="tech-card">
  <h3>Plotly.js</h3>
  <p>Creates responsive, interactive plots for each numerical method.</p>
</div>

<div class="tech-card">
  <h3>math.js</h3>
  <p>Parses and evaluates user-entered mathematical expressions in the browser.</p>
</div>

</div>

</div>
